claim: YOLO demand router implements evidence scope discrimination with project, external, user, and unknown categories; project facts require project-scoped evidence from code/tests/docs/config/logs/artifacts with repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four scope categories: project, external, user, unknown. DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false and result_schema=yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1.
evidence.why: Schema definitions establish the core evidence scope contract.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope infers scope from record fields; project scope requires path field or project_* source; external scope requires URL or external_* source.
evidence.why: Scope inference logic validates evidence classification.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks if project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete repo-relative paths; external-only evidence triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.

buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions with hard rules: boundary prohibits target project file modification, allowed artifact root, every evidence record must include scope, project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths.
evidence.why: Agent prompt contracts enforce evidence scope discipline.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots detect project file mutations; allowed write roots exclude project files; boundary violations become readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Boundary audit prevents evidence agents from modifying target project files.

evidence.summary: Test external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts; readiness blocks with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE.
evidence.why: Test validates external evidence cannot prove project state.

evidence.summary: Test project evidence requires concrete project locator; missing path triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test validates project evidence must have file locators.

evidence.summary: Demand Router doctrine specifies hard triggers for careful route; evidence agent protocols distinguish explorer/cross-checker/verifier; evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative paths; external evidence is background only.
evidence.why: Documentation codifies evidence scope philosophy and contract.

risks: Evidence agents could misclassify scope or omit required paths, causing false blockers or allowing unverified claims.
Boundary snapshot mechanism could miss mutations in very large repos or when allowed roots overlap project paths.

result.notes: Evidence scope discrimination is implemented across schema, router, dispatch, tests, and docs. Project facts require project-scoped evidence with paths; external-only evidence is correctly blocked from satisfying existing-project claims. Boundary audit prevents mutations during evidence gathering.
